key_insight: Droplet fusion and droplet shape recovery both arise from capillarity, but viscoelastic droplets relax through an additional stress-relaxation timescale that makes fusion a multistage process distinct from simple shape recovery.
engineering_relevance: For modeling biomolecular condensates and soft materials, the results show that purely viscous laws can mispredict relaxation rates and that constitutive viscoelasticity is needed to capture fusion dynamics and dissipation.
method: Analytical small-deformation theory for shape recovery
Axisymmetric finite-element simulations using the Oldroyd-B model
Comparison of Newtonian and viscoelastic droplets under matched conditions
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic theory-simulation comparisons showing single- or multi-exponential recovery for shape relaxation and deviations from stretched-exponential fusion behavior in viscoelastic regimes, with exterior fluid increasing hydrodynamic dissipation.
watch_item: Oldroyd-B is a simplified constitutive model, so quantitative predictions may depend on how well a real condensate matches its single-relaxation-time assumptions.
paper_title: Droplet Fusion as a Relaxation Process: Comparison with Shape Recovery of Newtonian and Viscoelastic Droplets
